payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields typically capture: token counts, provider/model identifiers, estimated price, and latency percentiles. Token counts should include input/prompt tokens, output/completion tokens, and total tokens, so usage and cost can be audited. Provider/model IDs should record the serving provider and exact model identifier used, because pricing, latency, and behavior vary by provider and model version. Estimated price should be computed from the recorded token counts and the provider/model pricing table, ideally split into input cost, output cost, and total estimated cost. Latency fields p50, p90, and p95 summarize response-time distribution across eval runs: p50 is the median latency, p90 is the latency below which 90% of runs completed, and p95 is the latency below which 95% of runs completed. These fields allow comparison of correctness, cost, and performance across model/provider configurations.
citations: Input request: Explain required eval accounting fields: token counts, provider/model IDs, estimated price, latency p50 p90 p95.
payload.decision: No durable memory should be committed; this is a contextual/debug explanation request, not a user preference or stable fact about the user.
